methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $3.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: xAI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $250B

private.name: Crusoe Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B+

private.name: SandboxAQ
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $5.6B

private.name: WEKA
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Modal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $1.1B

data.narrative:
Ayar Labs, an AI & Software company at Series E stage, is evaluated against a comp universe of 9 private frontier-tech peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir). The peer set for stage-matched valuation includes xAI, Crusoe Energy, SandboxAQ, WEKA, and Modal, spanning a wide range from $1.1B to $250B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis applies a typical 1.4x step-up multiplier to the company's last post-money valuation of $3.8B, implying $5.2B. Peer-Median analysis across five same-sector, same-stage companies yields a median of $5.6B, though the peer set exhibits high dispersion (min $1.1B, max $250B), reflecting heterogeneity in market positioning and capital intensity within the AI software sector. Both methods are applicable and produce convergent results.

The triangulated valuation range is $4.5B to $6.4B, with a median of $5.4B. This is modestly above the current $3.75B valuation, suggesting modest upside relative to the last financing round. The primary uncertainty is the wide dispersion in peer valuations, particularly the $250B outlier (xAI), which limits the predictive power of peer-median approaches in this nascent sector. Capital efficiency metrics and revenue growth rates would strengthen conviction in range placement within this band.
